ddls:
CREATE TABLE "job_functions" (
  "job_function_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each job function */,
  "job_function_name" TEXT /* Name of the job function */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the job function */,
  "industry_sector" TEXT /* Industry sector to which the job function belongs */,
  "demand_level" TEXT /* Current demand level for the job function (e.g., high, medium, low) */,
  "growth_trend" TEXT /* Growth trend of the job function over the years (e.g., increasing, stable, decreasing) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("job_function_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "employment_outcomes" (
  "outcome_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each employment outcome */,
  "job_function_id" INTEGER /* ID of the job function */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year of the employment outcome data */,
  "region" TEXT /* Geographical region where the data is collected */,
  "employment_rate" TEXT /* Percentage of graduates seeking employment who accept jobs within 3 months */,
  "median_salary" TEXT /* Median salary for the job function */,
  "mean_salary" TEXT /* Mean salary for the job function */,
  "low_base_salary" TEXT /* Lowest base salary for the job function */,
  "high_base_salary" TEXT /* Highest base salary for the job function */,
  "number_of_graduates" INTEGER /* Number of graduates contributing to the employment rate */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("outcome_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employment_outcomes_job_function_id FOREIGN KEY ("job_function_id") REFERENCES job_functions ("job_function_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "graduates" (
  "graduate_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each graduate */,
  "job_function_id" INTEGER /* ID of the job function the graduate is employed in */,
  "graduation_year" INTEGER /* Year the graduate completed their degree */,
  "degree_type" TEXT /* Type of degree (e.g., Bachelor's, Master's, PhD) */,
  "university_department" TEXT /* Department from which the graduate received their degree */,
  "salary" TEXT /* Salary of the graduate */,
  "employment_status" TEXT /* Employment status of the graduate (e.g., employed, unemployed) */,
  "employment_start_date" TEXT /* Date the graduate started their employment */,
  "company_name" TEXT /* Name of the company where the graduate is employed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("graduate_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_graduates_job_function_id FOREIGN KEY ("job_function_id") REFERENCES job_functions ("job_function_id")
)
